description:
You are an incident responder who has just received a copy of the system-wide SSH log.
/home/user/logs/auth.log already exists on disk.

Goal: produce a concise text report that management can read.

Steps you must perform
1.  Scan /home/user/logs/auth.log and count how many lines contain the exact phrase  
    Failed password
2.  Extract every IP address that appears on those same “Failed password” lines, 
    remove duplicates, and sort them in ascending order.
3.  Create the directory /home/user/incident (if it does not yet exist) and write a **new** file called  
    /home/user/incident/ssh_failures_2023-05-15.txt   with the following strict layout (including the blank line after the first line):

    Total failed SSH login attempts: <NUMBER>

    List of offending IP addresses:
    <IP-1>
    <IP-2>
    …

    •  Replace <NUMBER> with the integer count.  
    •  Replace the placeholder IP lines with the sorted list you obtained, one IP per line.  
    •  Ensure the file ends with a trailing newline.

Constraints
•  Use only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities such as grep, cut, awk, sort, uniq, wc, etc.  
•  The complete task can be done with a small pipeline of 2–3 commands; avoid writing custom scripts.  
•  Do not modify /home/user/logs/auth.log.  
•  The automated checker will look only at the contents of /home/user/incident/ssh_failures_2023-05-15.txt and at the path of that file.

When you are done, display the contents of the newly created report in the terminal so that a human reviewer can confirm the results.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/logs/auth.log
────────────────────────
May 15 10:22:34 server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 51122 ssh2
May 15 10:23:01 server sshd[12346]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.5 port 42311 ssh2
May 15 10:24:07 server sshd[12347]: Accepted password for user1 from 192.0.2.7 port 38712 ssh2
May 15 10:25:12 server sshd[12348]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.5 port 42319 ssh2
May 15 10:26:14 server sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user test from 198.51.100.55 port 53345 ssh2
May 15 10:27:18 server sshd[12350]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 51159 ssh2
May 15 10:28:30 server sshd[12351]: Accepted password for user2 from 203.0.113.8 port 42229 ssh2
May 15 10:29:02 server sshd[12352]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.8 port 42235 ssh2

Expected result file:
/home/user/incident/ssh_failures_2023-05-15.txt
──────────────────────────────────────────────
Total failed SSH login attempts: 6

List of offending IP addresses:
192.168.1.10
198.51.100.55
203.0.113.5
203.0.113.8

(there is a trailing newline after the last IP)

Permissions:
The agent runs as the normal user “user”, who owns /home/user/.  The /home/user/logs directory and its contents are readable.  /home/user/incident does not exist at start; it must be created by the agent and should end up owned by user with mode 700, but mode is not strictly tested.

Automated tests:
1. Confirm /home/user/incident/ssh_failures_2023-05-15.txt exists.
2. Verify its exact byte-for-byte content matches the block shown above, including the blank line and final newline.
name: task_000000_13f9c582
